finding: Empty/whitespace-only code short-circuited the effect and fell through to a perpetual 'Rendering diagram…' placeholder that never resolves.
The cancelled flag guarded setState but not the in-flight mermaid.render; a rapid source/theme change reused the SAME stable id, risking a DOM-id collision between overlapping renders.

mermaid.initialize sets a global singleton theme; two instances with different isDark could interleave. Rejected: the app has ONE global theme, so isDark is identical across all instances at any instant — the divergent-theme scenario cannot occur.
URL.revokeObjectURL ran synchronously right after a.click(), which can abort the download before the browser reads the blob (Firefox).
Error cleanup removed only '#d'+id; mermaid may name its stray temp node with the plain id, leaking an orphan node into <body>.
A mermaid parse throw is caught internally and rendered as an inline error rather than propagating — correctly keeps the surrounding StreamdownErrorBoundary from blanking the whole message.

Model-provided source is injected via dangerouslySetInnerHTML relying on mermaid strict-mode. Rejected as a regression: securityLevel:'strict' runs mermaid's bundled DOMPurify over the SVG — identical trust level to the Streamdown built-in mermaid path this replaces; no new surface introduced.
The injected diagram container had no role/label, presenting an unlabeled graphic to assistive tech.
The render-failure error block and the rendering placeholder lacked role=alert/aria-live, so SR users were not notified on failure/progress.
TEST-5 asserted only the .svg filename and never read the download bytes, so an empty/broken SVG would still pass.
TEST-4 reads the clipboard, which is Chromium-only + focus-dependent. Rejected: the visual Playwright project runs a single Desktop Chrome project, where clipboard-read is granted and reliable.

The diagram renders eagerly on every source/theme change even in source mode. Rejected: intentional per DEC-6 — instant toggling AND download-SVG must work while viewing source, so the SVG is kept ready in both modes.
memo uses default shallow prop comparison; all reactive inputs (code, isIncomplete, defaultMode) are primitives so the comparison is correct and re-renders fire on real changes.

Verified that passing plugins={{renderers}} does NOT disable Streamdown's default code(shiki)/math(katex): in the 2.5.0 dist those are additive (`g!=null&&g.math&&…`), and the pre-existing chat code passed NO plugins yet math/code worked — so a renderers-only plugin object cannot regress them.

CustomRenderer contract satisfied — component consumes {code,isIncomplete} from Streamdown; language/meta are accepted-but-unused; non-mermaid fences are untouched (renderer only claims language:'mermaid').
Mirrors the sibling MarkdownTable.tsx (card chrome, data-streamdown marker, ghost-icon toolbar, testid convention) and uses kit Segmented/Button per their documented APIs (Spec-B ghost cluster).
Pure client-side render of already-delivered markdown; no permission gate is bypassed or introduced.

User-facing strings are hardcoded English ('Copied!', 'Failed to copy', 'Empty diagram', tooltips) — consistent with the app-wide convention (MessageActions, MarkdownTable); no i18n framework exists to route through.
